summary:
The stoppage of venlafaxine commonly leads to anxiety and insomnia due to a known risk profile; therefore, it often results in patient-reported symptoms necessitating careful dose adjustments upon discontinuing treatment. To prevent severe discomfort during drug cessation, healthcare providers should consider implementing supervised weaning protocols when stopping venlafaxine therapy. ```plaintext
The stoppage of venlafaxine frequently triggers anxiety and insomnia, leading to significant distress requiring careful dosage reductions. Healthcare professionals must implement monitored tapering strategies to minimize these withdrawal symptoms effectively.
```
